message:
fix(messaging): re-insert queued pull request on dispatch error paths in FabricConsumer

dispatch_next_pull() popped the queued pull request before attempting
schedule_delivery() and pinned-client validation. If either path
failed, the request was silently dropped — the caller received an
error but the request was lost from the queue permanently.

Fixes:
- Re-insert the queued request before returning PinnedClientTicketMismatch
  so the caller can retry with a correct ticket
- Wrap schedule_delivery() in a match and re-insert on Err before
  propagating the error
- Preserve original enqueued_at_tick on re-enqueue for tail-available
  waiting so expiry deadlines are measured from first enqueue
- Remove unnecessary mut on queued binding
- Remove ticket.expect() panic surface (the ticket presence is now
  checked via let-chain guard)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
